Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

Eileen Collins on what it takes to become Space Shuttle Commander

In this episode of the Space Minds podcast, host David Ariosto speaks with Eileen Collins, retired NASA astronaut, Air Force colonel and the first woman to pilot the Space Shuttle […] The post Eileen Collins on what it takes to become Space Shuttle Commander appeared first on SpaceNews.

Collins's career trajectory from Air Force pilot to Space Shuttle Commander demonstrates that mastery of complex aerospace systems and unwavering commitment to excellence remain the defining prerequisites for commanding crewed spaceflight operations.

Press Release: Tickets on sale for United’s 787-9 with Elevated interior

Starting today, travelers can book seats on United’s most premium international aircraft yet — the 787-9 Dreamliner with the United Elevated interior. Unveiled last May, this new aircraft has been redesigned from nose-to-tail with 99 total premium seats — the highest of percentage among U.S. carr...

United's 787-9 with Elevated interior maximizes premium seat density at 99 seats, positioning the airline to capture higher-yield revenue on long-haul international routes through product differentiation.

Luke Maximobell is a pioneering DIY drone engineer known for his remarkable achievements in drone

DIY drone engineering innovations like Bell's antigravity propulsion concepts demonstrate how grassroots innovation can push boundaries in lightweight aircraft design, potentially influencing future commercial and eVTOL development.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

American Bald Eagle at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center

An American bald eagle flies away from its nest and tree at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida on Friday, March 13, 2026. Bald eagle nesting surveys across NASA Kennedy, Merritt Island National Wildlife Refuge, and Canaveral National Seashore are conducted annually to document the number of b...

NASA Kennedy Space Center's active bald eagle nesting population demonstrates successful wildlife management integration with critical aerospace launch infrastructure operations.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

How NASA is Collecting Explosion Data for Next Generation Rockets

Commercial launch providers continue to advance propulsion technology with a renewed focus on liquid oxygen and methane propelled rockets and spacecraft. As systems grow in scale, carrying millions of pounds of propellant, so too does the responsibility to fully understand the safety profile. Eng...

NASA's systematic collection of explosion data from scaled-up liquid oxygen and methane propulsion systems is critical for establishing safety standards that commercial launch providers must meet as they scale next-generation rockets to unprecedented propellant capacities.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 19, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires aviation organizations to systematically develop and implement controls while documenting their acceptance of residual risks that cannot be eliminated, ensuring regulatory compliance under FAA Part 5 requirements.

Biomimetic drone technology disguised as seagulls represents an emerging application of autonomous systems for coastal surveillance, introducing novel regulatory and privacy considerations for aviation authorities monitoring airspace.

Safety Mar 19, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern aviation's shift from reactive accident investigation to proactive Safety Management Systems represents a fundamental transformation in how the industry identifies and mitigates risks before incidents occur.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA Laser Reflecting Instrument Makes GPS Satellite More Accurate

A NASA laser reflecting technology that will aid Global Positioning System (GPS) accuracy is now operational as of March 9. The instrument, known as a laser retroreflector array, or LRA, launched aboard GPS III SV-09, the ninth of U.S. Space Force’s Block III Global Positioning System satellites,...

NASA's laser retroreflector array on GPS III SV-09 enhances positioning accuracy through space-based laser tracking, providing aviation with more precise navigation and positioning capabilities for flight operations and safety systems.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 19, 2026

Airlines demand EU leaders set 21-day notice for air traffic control strikes

An airline body representing some of Europe’s largest and most influential carriers is demanding that EU leaders set… The post Airlines demand EU leaders set 21-day notice for air traffic control strikes appeared first on AeroTime.

European airlines are seeking regulatory intervention to mitigate operational disruptions by requiring advance notice of air traffic control strikes, highlighting the critical vulnerability of commercial aviation to labor actions in fragmented ATC systems.

See AeroTime Hub →

Regulatory Mar 19, 2026

FCC Exempts Four Drones From Import Ban

March action follows January temporary exemptions tied to broader foreign UAS restrictions.

The FCC's selective drone import exemptions signal a regulatory pathway for manufacturers to navigate broader UAS restrictions, though the criteria and timeline for broader relief remain unclear for the industry.

See AVweb →

French A400M makes first-ever landing on Arctic sea ice in Greenland

A French Air and Space Force A400M Atlas has landed on Arctic sea ice in northern Greenland, in what France’s… The post French A400M makes first-ever landing on Arctic sea ice in Greenland appeared first on AeroTime.

The A400M's successful Arctic sea ice landing demonstrates the tactical versatility of modern military transport aircraft in extreme environments, expanding operational capability for polar logistics and emergency response missions.

Go behind the scenes at EAA AirVenture with the FAA's Aviation Safety organization. Meet the people who help ensure safety at this event and provide essential information resources to the general aviation community.

The FAA's visible safety presence at EAA AirVenture demonstrates the agency's commitment to proactive engagement with general aviation stakeholders, offering real-time educational resources that improve safety outcomes across the community.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

Robinson Helicopter Details New Unmanned Business Unit, Cargo UAS Project With Sikorsky

Robinson Helicopter on March 9 announced the launch of its new Robinson Unmanned business unit, and detailed a new unmanned cargo helicopter offering utilizing Sikorsky’s autonomy capability. With Robinson Unmanned, […] The post Robinson Helicopter Details New Unmanned Business Unit, Cargo UAS Pr...

Robinson Helicopter's entry into the unmanned cargo market through a strategic partnership with Sikorsky signals a major aerospace shift toward autonomous logistics solutions and represents a significant diversification opportunity for traditional rotorcraft manufacturers.

Military Mar 19, 2026

A-10s Now Hunting Iranian Fast Attack Craft in the Strait of Hormuz

U.S. Air Force A-10s in maritime attack role are hunting small Iranian fast attack boats that could threaten shipping or naval forces in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. Air Force A-10 Thundebolt II jets supporting operation Epic Fury have started flying a different type of mission. As explained by...

The A-10 Thunderbolt II is expanding its operational role beyond traditional close air support to conduct maritime patrol and anti-surface missions against fast attack craft, demonstrating the platform's continued relevance in evolving regional security operations.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA to Cover Progress 94 Spacecraft Launch, Space Station Docking

NASA will provide live coverage of the launch and docking of a Roscosmos cargo spacecraft carrying about three tons of food, fuel, and supplies for the crew aboard the International Space Station. The unpiloted Roscosmos Progress 94 resupply spacecraft is scheduled to launch at 7:59 a.m. EDT (4:5...

NASA's continued coordination with Roscosmos on ISS resupply missions demonstrates the resilience of international space partnerships despite geopolitical tensions, maintaining critical supply chains for orbital operations.

See NASA →

5 Airlines With The World's Quietest Business Class Cabins In 2026

These carriers offer exceptional premium products.

Airlines competing for premium passenger loyalty are investing heavily in cabin noise reduction technology, which has become a key differentiator in business class product offerings alongside traditional amenities.

Industry Mar 19, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through advanced technologies and integrated airline-airport-flight systems offers a critical pathway to accommodate projected air traffic growth while addressing environmental sustainability challenges.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 19, 2026

Airbus Built The A350F, But Why Hasn’t Boeing Produced A 787 Freighter?

Boeing is addressing the A350F with another aircraft.

Boeing's absence of a 787 freighter variant represents a strategic market gap that Airbus is exploiting with the A350F, reflecting divergent cargo fleet modernization strategies between manufacturers.

Airbus raises stakes in engine dispute with Pratt & Whitney: Reuters

Aircraft manufacturer Airbus is seeking damages against US engine maker Pratt & Whitney over delivery delays hampering production,… The post Airbus raises stakes in engine dispute with Pratt & Whitney: Reuters appeared first on AeroTime.

Airbus's damages claim against Pratt & Whitney signals escalating supply chain tensions that could reshape engine procurement strategies and force OEMs to diversify supplier relationships to mitigate production risks.

Boeing 777-9 Hits Massive FAA Milestone: Is The Long Wait Finally Over?

With this green light from the FAA, Boeing is one step closer to its goal of certifying its long-awaited 777-9 aircraft.

Boeing's 777-9 certification progress signals potential relief for airlines awaiting wide-body aircraft deliveries, though production ramp-up and supply chain stability remain critical variables for meeting pent-up market demand.

AeroVironment's $200 million acquisition of ESAero strategically expands its uncrewed systems manufacturing capacity and hybrid-electric propulsion capabilities, positioning the company for growth in the competitive defense and commercial UAS markets.

Commercial Mar 19, 2026

How Long Did American Airlines Operate The Boeing 747 For?

Find out how long American Airlines flew the Boeing 747 and what led to the early retirement of the iconic “Queen of the Skies.”

American Airlines' early retirement of the Boeing 747 reflects the industry-wide shift toward smaller, more fuel-efficient twin-engine aircraft that better matched evolving route networks and economics in the post-9/11 era.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class

Ten explorers are currently training at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston to become flight-eligible astronauts. Selected in 2025, the astronaut candidates are building the technical and operational skills needed for future missions to the International Space Station, the Moon, and eventually...

NASA's 2025 astronaut candidate class represents the future workforce for expanding human spaceflight operations across LEO, lunar, and deep space missions, requiring aviation and aerospace professionals to anticipate evolving training, vehicle integration, and mission support requirements.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA’s Roman Observatory Passes Final Major Prelaunch Tests

NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope team recently blasted the observatory with extreme sound, shook it, and listened to its electronic hum. Roman passed all three assessments, which aimed to confirm that the observatory will withstand launch conditions and function as expected in space. The ...

NASA's Roman Space Telescope successfully completed critical environmental qualification testing, demonstrating the rigorous validation processes that ensure aerospace mission success and reliability in the extreme conditions of space operations.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 19, 2026

How One Of The World's Most Notorious Fraudsters Posed As A Pilot To Fly For Free

Made extra-famous through the hollywood film ‘Catch Me If You Can’, legendary conman turned security consultant Frank Abagnale managed to fly for free aboard some 250 flights by posing as a deadheading pilot - a flight crew member being repositioned to another city for operational purposes. In a ...

Frank Abagnale's exploitation of deadheading protocols to fraudulently access commercial flights highlights critical vulnerabilities in crew authentication procedures that modern aviation security must continually address.

See Simple Flying →

Russian Su-30 briefly violates Estonian airspace near Vaindloo, NATO jets respond

A Russian Su-30 fighter jet briefly entered Estonian airspace near Vaindloo Island on March 18, 2026, prompting a… The post Russian Su-30 briefly violates Estonian airspace near Vaindloo, NATO jets respond appeared first on AeroTime.

Russian military aircraft continue probing NATO airspace boundaries in the Baltic region, requiring persistent NATO air defense responses and highlighting the operational tempo of European airspace management.

DJI Mini 4K now priced like a budget accessory

The DJI Mini 4K, one of the easiest, most beginner-friendly drones you can buy right now, has just dropped to its lowest price ever. And we’re not talking about a small discount deal. These are the kind of prices that make you double-check if it’s real. more…

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy on consumer drones continues to lower barriers to entry for recreational unmanned aircraft operations, potentially expanding the addressable market for drone services while creating regulatory compliance challenges for airspace management.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings

NASA is proposing a sharp increase in the rate of robotic lunar lander missions, a move that has excited but also puzzled the space community. The post NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings appeared first on SpaceNews.

NASA's acceleration of robotic lunar landing missions signals increased demand for specialized aerospace manufacturing, launch services, and avionics capabilities that will reshape supplier contracts across the aviation and aerospace sectors.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI

Finnish satellite manufacturer ReOrbit has signed a contract with asset-financing company SLI for two small geostationary orbit communications satellites. The post ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI appeared first on SpaceNews.

Small satellite manufacturers are increasingly securing commercial orders for GEO communications platforms, signaling growing demand for cost-effective alternatives to traditional large satellites in the geostationary market.

Everdrone raises SEK 36 million – enters commercial phase for drone services in emergency healthcare

Everdrone currently operates the world’s first operational drone service integrated into the emergency response chain

Everdrone's secured funding and commercial launch of integrated emergency healthcare drones represents a critical inflection point for proving unmanned aircraft viability in time-sensitive, regulated medical applications.

Drones Mar 19, 2026

uAvionix Introduces New ping978EC ADS-B Transceiver Enabling UK BVLOS UAS Operators to Meet Latest CAA Electronic Conspicuity Regulations

uAvionix, a leader in aviation communications, navigation, and surveillance data solutions, is excited to announce

uAvionix's ping978EC transceiver enables UK drone operators to achieve regulatory compliance for beyond-visual-line-of-sight operations through standardized ADS-B electronic conspicuity, expanding commercial UAS capabilities in controlled airspace.

NASA is proposing a sharp increase in the rate of robotic lunar lander missions, a move that has excited but also puzzled the space community. The post NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings appeared first on SpaceNews.

NASA's acceleration of robotic lunar landing missions signals increased demand for specialized aerospace manufacturing, launch services, and avionics capabilities that will reshape supplier contracts across the aviation and aerospace sectors.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI

Finnish satellite manufacturer ReOrbit has signed a contract with asset-financing company SLI for two small geostationary orbit communications satellites. The post ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI appeared first on SpaceNews.

Small satellite manufacturers are increasingly securing commercial orders for GEO communications platforms, signaling growing demand for cost-effective alternatives to traditional large satellites in the geostationary market.

Drones Mar 19, 2026

Everdrone raises SEK 36 million – enters commercial phase for drone services in emergency healthcare

Everdrone currently operates the world’s first operational drone service integrated into the emergency response chain

Everdrone's secured funding and commercial launch of integrated emergency healthcare drones represents a critical inflection point for proving unmanned aircraft viability in time-sensitive, regulated medical applications.

Drones Mar 19, 2026

uAvionix Introduces New ping978EC ADS-B Transceiver Enabling UK BVLOS UAS Operators to Meet Latest CAA Electronic Conspicuity Regulations

uAvionix, a leader in aviation communications, navigation, and surveillance data solutions, is excited to announce

uAvionix's ping978EC transceiver enables UK drone operators to achieve regulatory compliance for beyond-visual-line-of-sight operations through standardized ADS-B electronic conspicuity, expanding commercial UAS capabilities in controlled airspace.

Drones Mar 19, 2026

US drone ban has a growing exemption list

The United States has exempted four foreign-made drones from its sweeping import ban. See the latest additions to a list that’s quietly getting longer… more…

The US drone import ban's expanding exemption list signals potential policy flexibility that operators should monitor for regulatory changes affecting equipment procurement strategies.
